title: [InstCombine] Set flags when canonicalizing GEP indices
summary:
The generalization strategy transforms a single test case into a reusable pattern by abstracting constants to variables and extending support across multiple scenarios. Developers first identified the core optimization—comparing GEPs with different element types—and generalized it by replacing fixed indices (`2`, `10`) with symbolic variables (`%i`, `%j`). This enables the transformation to handle arbitrary index values.

They then expanded applicability by adding test cases for:
- Different address spaces (including `addrspacecast`)
- Various index types (`i16`, `i32`, `i64`)
- Nested struct/array GEPs
- Both `inbounds` and non-`inbounds` variants
- Edge cases like overflow and zero-length arrays

The key insight: the same underlying logic (comparing byte offsets via index scaling) works regardless of specific constants, address spaces, or index types. The patch ensures correct signed/unsigned extension/truncation based on GEP flags, preserving poison semantics. This approach turns a one-off optimization into a robust, widely applicable transformation.
